semanticSearchField: Botanical name: Forsythia × intermedia 'Lynwood Variety'. Preferred Common Name: forsythia 'Lynwood Variety'. Also known as: forsythia 'Lynwood'. Family: Oleaceae. Genus: Forsythia. Description: 'Lynwood Variety' is a vigorous medium-sized deciduous shrub, upright in growth, with narrowly ovate or sometimes trifoliate leaves. Flowers relatively large, bright yellow, in early spring. Genus information: Forsythia are deciduous shrubs with simple, occasionally lobed, leaves and short tubular yellow flowers with 4 spreading lobes, borne profusely before the leaves in spring. Cultivation: Grow in moist but well-drained soil in sun or partial shade. Characteristics: plant type: Shrub, height: 1.5-2.5 metres, spread: 1.5-2.5 metres, foliage: Deciduous, sunlight requirements: Full Sun, Partial Shade, soil type: Chalk, Clay, Loam, Sand, aspect: South-facing, East-facing, North-facing, West-facing, hardiness: H5 (hardy - cold winter), moisture requirements: Moist but well-drained, pH requirements: Acid, Alkaline, Neutral, habit: Bushy, time to full height: 5-10 years, exposure: Exposed, Sheltered. Colors and features: flowers: Yellow in Spring, foliage color: Green in Spring, Summer and  Autumn. Suggested uses: Banks and Slopes, Cottage/Informal Garden, Hedging/Screens, Coastal, Cut Flowers, Low Maintenance. Care information: pruning: Pruning group 2, propagation: Propagate by semi-hardwood cuttings, pest resistance: May be susceptible to birds eating the flower buds, disease resistance: May be susceptible to honey fungus and forsythia gall. Special features: awards: Award of Garden Merit
